{{Infobox character
|nom=March Hare
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

The March Hare is a resident known to the Cheshire Cat as mad. He maintains a tea party beneath a tree outside his house with the Hatter and the Dormouse as regular companions. Upon Alice's arrival at the table, he offered her wine, though only tea was available. The March Hare interrupts conversations when bored, as when he told Alice, "Suppose we change the subject. I'm getting tired of this," while yawning. He and his companions engage in what is described as a never-ending meal.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Hatter]]''' — acquaintance. The two are companions at the tea party, seated together at the table under a tree.

'''[[Alice]]''' — acquaintance. Alice joins the tea party uninvited, and the March Hare greets her with an invitation to sit and offers her wine.

'''[[Dormouse]]''' — acquaintance. The Dormouse sits between the March Hare and the Hatter at the tea party table.

''Evolution:''
* [[Hatter]] — Acquaintance (ch.7→ch.11)
* [[Dormouse]] — Acquaintance (ch.7→ch.11)
* [[Alice]] — Acquaintance (ch.7)
== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Secondary Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
